title: 19. Comparative genomics — evolutionary evidence for each truncation step 180 orthologs
desc: Evolutionary evidence for both truncation steps. Step 1 (N-terminal cut at 700): supported by Pfam domain boundary, fish length variation, and bird STRC absence. Step 2 (LRR-linker cut at 1075): is not at a Pfam boundary — the LRR domain extends to ~1100 — but AF3 interface preservation across species-level variation in LRR length (see fish orthologs) and the directly tested Ultra-Mini AF3 gates (§9–§11) justify the deeper cut. We checked ~180 STRC orthologs across vertebrates.
finding1: Among all Aves, only the North Island brown kiwi has a STRC gene (1,777 aa). Chickens, songbirds, and owls have no STRC gene at all — yet have excellent hearing (125 Hz - 8 kHz+). Stereocilin is not universally required for vertebrate hearing. It is specific to the mammalian OHC horizontal top connector / tectorial membrane attachment system.
finding2_title: Pfam boundary justifies step 1; step 2 validated directly by AF3
finding2: The Stereocilin LRR domain (PF21058) maps to human residues ~700-1100 across all annotated orthologs. The N-terminal ~700 residues contain no recognized Pfam domain — the first-pass Mini-STRC cut at 700 falls exactly at the domain boundary. Step 2 (Ultra-Mini, cut at 1075) excises the LRR linker inside PF21058. This is a more aggressive cut and is not justifiable by Pfam alone — instead it relies on the direct AF3 interface-preservation evidence in §9–§11, which shows the TMEM145 binding pocket sits entirely above residue 1075.
finding3_title: Fish STRC is longer, not shorter
finding3: Bony fish stereocilins range from 1,900-2,700 aa — up to 900 aa longer than human. The extra length is in the N-terminal region. This demonstrates the N-terminus tolerates massive size variation (±900 aa) across vertebrate evolution without losing function.
finding4_title: Dual-AAV papers converge on the Mini-STRC boundary
finding4: Bhatt et al. (2022, Science Advances) independently chose their dual-AAV split at Ser746/Cys747 to avoid disrupting structural domains. Iranfar et al. (2026) used a similar split. Two independent groups, designing for different purposes, identified the same first-pass boundary (~700) that AlphaFold 3's disorder profile predicts. Our Ultra-Mini goes beyond their boundary, but it's a single-vector design, so it doesn't need to obey the split-site constraints that force dual-AAV papers to stop at the Pfam edge.
conclusion: No vertebrate carries a naturally truncated STRC matching either our step-1 (700-1775) or step-2 (1075-1775) construct. But the convergent evidence — no N-terminal Pfam domain, bird STRC absence, fish LRR-length variation up to ±900 aa, and independent dual-AAV split-point selection at ~700 — supports the hypothesis that neither the N-terminal disordered region nor the middle LRR linker is essential for the C-terminal core's structural function. The Ultra-Mini-specific claim (LRR linker dispensable) is anchored on direct AF3 interface preservation rather than on evolutionary precedent.
